figures.caption: Fig. 1. Conceptual overview of BAT. The decoupled policy (blue) provides stable and disturbance-robust behaviors, while the coupled policy (red) enables agile and dynamic motions. BAT adaptively switches between them to achieve both stability and agility.
TABLE III COMPARISON OF MOTION TRACKING PERFORMANCE ACROSS DIVERSE STATIC AND DYNAMIC MOTIONS.

Fig. 5. Comparison of our method (BAT) with various switching strategies in simulation. Top: training set (in-distribution, 400 motion combinations). Bottom: test set (unseen transitions, 100 combinations). BAT denotes the full method. Opt-HRL uses option guidance with HRL, while Opt-HRL-i trains on individual motions without transition exposure. Opt-BC uses option guidance with behavior cloning only (no HRL), and HRL corresponds to unguided hierarchical RL. Additional baselines include GT-Opt (oracle option selection), Opt-Pred (option prediction), Heur (human heuristics), and fixed decoupled (πD) and coupled (πC ) policies.
